statement.en: Forest Structure and Species Composition of Flooded Forests in the Tonlé Sap Ecosystem, Cambodia
statement.summary: Abstract Flooded forests surrounding Tonlé Sap Lake represent one of the most ecologically important wetland forest systems in Southeast Asia. These forests play a crucial role in maintaining biodiversity, regulating hydrological processes, and supporting fisheries productivity in the Mekong Basin. The structure and species composition of flooded forests are shaped primarily by seasonal flooding regimes, soil conditions, and human disturbances. This review synthesizes existing literature on the structural characteristics, dominant species, and ecological dynamics of flooded forests in the Tonlé Sap floodplain. The findings indicate that these forests are dominated by flood-tolerant tree species with relatively low canopy height but high ecological productivity. However, increasing pressure

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a literature review/synthesis paper on established ecological phenomena (flooded forest structure, species composition). The core empirical observations are TRUE (flooded forests exist, are biodiverse, flood-tolerant species dominate), but the paper itself is dated 2026 (future date, impossible), marked preprint-stage ('review synthesizes'), and the abstract is incomplete ('However, increasing pressure...'). Classification as FLOWING reflects: (1) provisional/incomplete submission state, (2) future publication date creating temporal inconsistency, (3) synthesis work pending peer validation, (4) incomplete abstract preventing full evaluation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 洪水と適応種の共鳴が人間の圧力に抗して生産性を保つ神秘的なバランス
